I’m trying to read the entire long list before the prize is announced in June. I’m about halfway through, and have read 3 of the 6 on the shortlist.

Flashlight: good but not great; needed to be several chapters shorter but wrapped up well.

The Mercy Step: great; despite some heavy themes the story is told with a lightness and slight humour that makes it enjoyable.

Heart the Lover: great; thought I would hate it as it’s billed as a romance but the way the story spans a lifetime without getting bogged down in any boring bits works really well.

The only one of these I've read is The Correspondent and it was my favorite read of last year, although it doesn't scream "prize list". 

I have Kingfisher checked out from the library right now and am hoping I can get to it before it's due back! I feel like a lot of others will want to read it now that it's shortlisted.
I go weekly. I was just at the library today. I borrow lots of books and movies too.
My library doesn’t even have late fees anymore. Libraries kick ass.

Well first of all I think it's very unfair when people rate ongoing shows and finished shows. I see this a lot with House of the Dragon compared to Game of Thrones. If Game of Thrones was releasing today, people would complain about it in the same exact way - but when the show finishes and you see the final product - even with awful finale, most people will still appreciate it much more.

Then another similar thing is that there's nothing that's 100% and nothing that's 0%. If you rate a show like that you're telling me that you cannot see over your bias or that you are dismissing too many elements to be reliable. A show is not just the writing or directing and let's take Game of Thrones again - Season 8 has one of the worst writings we have all seen but the production design, cinematography and all these things should still be enough to appreciate that and if you're rating it with 1/10 because of bad writing then you directly dismiss all that makes a TV show different from a book.

I personally rate episodes and seasons as stand-alone thing but always keep open doors for what the next episode or next season can offer and how this episode/season contributes to that. Unless I find it unwatchable so I just stop watching
Would interest me too

Overall 7/10.
This is actually really relatable. Your emotional state can absolutely change how a book lands, especially with character-driven stories.

I have not personally noticed this for my cycle specifically because I never felt like my period really affected my mental state much (it's just bad month round lol), but I think just in general it's very normal that how you feel about a book or anything really is affected by your emotions and well-being.
You should finish the book stoned and comeback and report.
